message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>After the major exponential phase, the Covid-19 pandemic is on a milder rate of increase globally, and past the peak in many countries. In this phase a marked systematic weekly periodicity in the Covid-19 attack has become clear, the study of which could be useful for the long term strategy to deal with the virus and the pandemic. The most important aspect of this strong weekly modulation is its global nature, phase-coherent over most of globe, independent of the geographical location. The fact that the same periodicity is now evident even those countries Asia and Africa, where it was absent or not prominent earlier, suggests the possibility that the pattern could be an early indicator for significant community transmission. The global coherent periodicity with a time scale that agrees with the time scales of virus incubation and infection may be important in tracking the long term interaction of the virus with the host.</jats:p>
title: Globally Coherent Weekly Periodicity in the Covid-19 Pandemic

reference.unstructured: Covid data is compiled at the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control (www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/publications-data).
The grapher tool and the data at the site ourworldindata.org is used for the graphs in this paper.
Secondary checks on the data was done with data and plots at the site www.worldometers.info/coronavirus
H. I. Freedman , Deterministic Mathematical Models in Population Ecol- ogy (Marcel Dekker, N.Y. 1980).
F. Brauer and C. Castillo-Chavez , Mathematical Models in Population Biology and Epidemiology (Springer, 2012).

reference.article-title: Turning Points And Relaxation Oscillation Cycles in Simple Epidemic Models
